title: Drama TV
subtitle: Prestige drama, workplace ensemble, character-first writing. The HBO/AMC/Sorkin/Showtime canon.

items.title: The West Wing
items.seasons: S1-S4 are Sorkin (loved); S5-S7 are post-Sorkin under John Wells
items.pitch: The Bartlet White House. Sorkin's masterpiece — workplace ensemble plus political idealism plus dialogue at his peak.

critics.quote: Won 26 Emmys including four consecutive Outstanding Drama Series for S1-S4. Routinely cited among the greatest American TV series.

items.title: The Newsroom
items.pitch: Sorkin's HBO show. Behind-the-scenes of a cable news anchor and his team trying to do a better newscast. The opening monologue is famous.

critics.quote: Polarizing critically — strong defenders and strong detractors. Won three Emmys.

sections.name: II. High Priority — HBO Crime-Drama Pillar
sections.desc: You loved Sopranos territory and True Detective S1. Direct fits.

items.title: Mad Men
items.whyPriority: AMC's prestige peak. 1960s NYC ad agency. Slow-burn character drama at its most rigorous. Routinely top-three on every great-American-TV list.
items.pitch: Don Draper. Sterling Cooper. The decade that changed America rendered through a Madison Avenue ad firm.

critics.quote: Four consecutive Outstanding Drama Emmys. Generally considered the AMC-era prestige drama equal to any HBO production.

items.title: Breaking Bad
items.whyPriority: If you haven't seen this, it's the highest-priority drama TV recommendation possible. Tighter structure than nearly any TV ever made. The 'Ozymandias' episode is among the great hours of television.
items.pitch: Albuquerque chemistry teacher diagnosed with cancer cooks meth to provide for his family.

critics.quote: Holds the highest aggregate critical score of any TV series in modern review history.

items.title: Six Feet Under
items.whyPriority: HBO's other turn-of-the-century masterpiece alongside Sopranos. Family-run funeral home in LA. The series finale is widely considered the greatest finale in television.
items.pitch: The Fishers run a Los Angeles funeral home. Each episode begins with a death.

critics.quote: Nine Emmys. Series finale 'Everyone's Waiting' tops most-greatest-finale lists.

items.title: Halt and Catch Fire
items.whyPriority: AMC, ended cleanly, badly underwatched at the time. Four characters in tech from 1983 PC clones to 1990s internet. The S2-S4 stretch is one of the great character-arc trajectories in TV.
items.pitch: 1980s Texas. A salesman wants to clone the IBM PC. Across four seasons the show follows him and his collaborators through Silicon Valley's birth.

critics.quote: Critical darling that never found audience. Routinely cited as the most underwatched great show of its era.

items.title: Rectify
items.whyPriority: SundanceTV. Almost no one saw it. Routinely listed by serious TV critics among the greatest American dramas. Slow, devastating, beautiful.
items.pitch: A man released from death row after 19 years tries to return to small-town Georgia life.

critics.quote: Metacritic among the highest-rated dramas in the site's history. Audience never matched the reviews.

sections.name: III. Already Watched
sections.desc: On record from your viewing history.

items.title: Studio 60 on the Sunset Strip
items.pitch: Sorkin's failed follow-up. Cross-listed in Comedy TV.

items.title: Nurse Jackie
items.dir: Liz Brixius, Linda Wallem, Evan Dunsky
items.pitch: Edie Falco as a NYC ER nurse with an opioid addiction. Showtime half-hour drama with comedic texture.

critics.quote: Falco won the Emmy for Lead Actress in a Comedy in 2010 — controversial because the show is not a comedy.

items.title: The Riches
items.pitch: Eddie Izzard and Minnie Driver as Irish Travelers who steal the identities of a wealthy family. Tragic that it was cancelled mid-arc.

critics.quote: Critical favorite. Izzard's first major dramatic role.

items.title: American Gods
items.seasons: Cancelled mid-arc; Gaiman novel ending never reached
items.pitch: Neil Gaiman adaptation. Bryan Fuller's first two seasons are visually astonishing; S3 collapsed after Fuller departure.

critics.quote: S1 critically acclaimed; quality declined with each showrunner change.

items.title: House MD
items.pitch: Misanthropic genius diagnostician solves medical mysteries while alienating everyone around him. Hugh Laurie and the case-of-the-week-with-Vicodin-subplot template that defined network drama for a decade.

critics.note: A character study disguised as a procedural.

critics.quote: Most-watched TV show in the world during peak years (2008). Laurie won six SAG/Globe awards combined.

items.title: The Boys
items.pitch: Amazon. Garth Ennis adaptation. Superheroes are awful corporate-owned monsters; ordinary people fight back.

critics.quote: Multiple Emmy nominations. Considered the prestige-superhero antithesis to MCU.

items.title: Landman
items.pitch: Sheridan's Texas oil show. Billy Bob Thornton anchors. Sheridan does Texas-elite-and-blue-collar like nobody else.

critics.quote: Largest cable drama premiere of 2024. Critically warm.

sections.name: V. High Priority — Recommended (Ended)
sections.desc: Strongest probable fits given your taste signal.

items.title: Deadwood
items.whyPriority: HBO Western. David Milch's Shakespearean-prose dialogue. Ian McShane's Al Swearengen is among the great TV characters.
items.pitch: 1870s Dakota mining town. Lawless. Profane. Characters argue ethics and economics in language that reads like Elizabethan blank verse.

critics.quote: Widely cited among the greatest American TV series. Eight Emmys.

items.title: Friday Night Lights
items.whyPriority: Texas high school football show that's actually about a marriage and a town. If you're a Texan watching shows about Texas you should not skip this.
items.pitch: Coach Eric Taylor and his wife Tami in Dillon, Texas. Football is the entry point; marriage and community are the substance.

critics.quote: Critical darling never matched by audience. Final season won the Peabody.

items.title: Show Me a Hero
items.whyPriority: David Simon (The Wire) on 1980s Yonkers housing desegregation. Six episodes. Oscar Isaac as the mayor. Direct fit for All The President's Men + West Wing political-process intersection.
items.pitch: 1987 Yonkers. A 28-year-old mayor inherits a federal court order to build public housing in white neighborhoods. The political costs play out across his term.

critics.quote: Critical praise from the start. Routinely cited as among the strongest miniseries of its decade.

items.title: Treme
items.whyPriority: Simon's post-Katrina New Orleans show. Slower than The Wire, music-saturated, a meditation rather than a procedural.
items.pitch: New Orleans residents rebuild after Hurricane Katrina across four years. Music is the through-line.

critics.quote: Critical favorite. Audience smaller than The Wire's. Beloved by Simon devotees.

items.title: Boardwalk Empire
items.whyPriority: HBO Prohibition-era Atlantic City crime drama. Steve Buscemi anchors. Considered the strongest tier-2 HBO drama (just below Sopranos/Wire).
items.pitch: Nucky Thompson runs Atlantic City during Prohibition. Real historical figures (Capone, Luciano, Rothstein) cycle through.

critics.quote: Twenty Emmys across run. Pilot directed by Scorsese remains a benchmark.

items.title: Succession
items.whyPriority: Logan Roy's media empire and his children jockeying to inherit it. Black-comic but structurally a tragedy. Won Outstanding Drama three times.
items.pitch: Aging media mogul + four awful children + an empire too big to share. Shakespearean.

critics.quote: Routinely cited among the greatest TV dramas of the streaming era.

items.title: Ozark
items.whyPriority: Often described as Breaking Bad with more institutional dread. Marty Byrde launders cartel money in the Missouri Ozarks. Laura Linney's Wendy is the real center.
items.pitch: Chicago financial advisor relocates his family to the Ozarks to launder money for a Mexican cartel.

critics.quote: Multiple Emmy wins for Bateman and Garner.

items.title: The Americans
items.whyPriority: Cross-listed in Spy TV. The marriage drama is what makes it a Drama; the Cold War espionage is texture. If Sopranos appeals, this is the next-tier prestige-drama-with-genre-engine.
items.pitch: Two KGB officers planted as a married couple in 1980s Washington DC suburbs.

critics.quote: Final season widely considered one of the best closing seasons in TV.

items.title: Better Things
items.whyPriority: Pamela Adlon as a single mother and working actress in LA. Categorized as comedy by Emmys but is dramatic in everything but length. Adlon took over as showrunner after CK's removal in 2017.
items.pitch: Sam Fox raises three daughters alone. Half-hour. Looser structure than most prestige drama; episodes feel like life rather than story.

critics.quote: Critically beloved. Adlon won Peabody and was four-time Emmy nominee.

items.title: Mr. Robot
items.whyPriority: Cross-listed in Sci-Fi TV (you may already have it tracked there). Hacker drama, structurally inventive, ended cleanly. Drama-first, sci-fi-adjacent.
items.pitch: NYC cybersecurity engineer / vigilante hacker plans to take down the largest corporation in the world.

items.title: Justified
items.whyPriority: Elmore Leonard adaptation. Timothy Olyphant as a US Marshal in eastern Kentucky. Smart procedural-and-character hybrid.
items.pitch: Raylan Givens. Cowboy boots. Backwoods crime. Rocking dialogue.

critics.quote: Critical favorite throughout run. Followed by Justified: City Primeval (2023, limited).

items.title: Fargo (TV)
items.seasons: Each season standalone; S1, S2, S5 considered strongest
items.pitch: Cross-listed in Crime TV. Noah Hawley's Coen-toned anthology. Each season a self-contained Coens-flavored crime story.

critics.quote: Won Outstanding Limited Series twice. Considered the Coens' best TV adaptation.

items.title: True Detective S1
items.seasons: S1 only — your love. S2-S4 anthology, varying quality.
items.pitch: Cross-listed in Crime TV. Marked Loved per your record.

items.title: The Wire
items.seasons: S1 in progress. S2 Polarizing, S3-S4 transcendent, S5 weakest.
items.pitch: Cross-listed in Crime TV. Currently watching.

items.title: The Sopranos
items.pitch: Cross-listed in Crime TV. Queued.
